sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: BridgeBio Pharma stock hits all-time high at 78.69 USD
feed.summary: BridgeBio Pharma (BBIO) stock has reached an all-time high of $78.69, reflecting a 127.81% total return over the past year and a significant 65.35% surge in the last six months. The company, with a market capitalization of $15.08 billion, shows strong investor confidence despite a reported loss per share over the last year, attributed to robust performance of its flagship product Attruby and a healthy financial position with a current ratio of 3.88. Analysts anticipate continued sales growth and have issued optimistic price targets, with TD Cowen reiterating a Buy rating and Morgan Stanley initiating an Overweight rating.

feed.title: Kroger appoints new SVP of retail divisions
feed.summary: Kroger has promoted Victor Smith, former president of its Atlanta Division, to Senior Vice President of Retail Divisions. This move is part of several executive changes across the company, including other presidential appointments and retirements, as Kroger prepares to name a permanent CEO in Q1 2026. Smith, who started as a courtesy clerk in 1983, will work alongside two other senior VPs of retail divisions.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank Keeps a Hold Rating on General Mills, Inc. (GIS)
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has reiterated its "Hold" rating on General Mills, Inc. (GIS) and raised its price target from $50 to $51. This decision follows General Mills' strong fiscal second-quarter earnings, which surpassed analyst expectations due to continued consumer preference for at-home eating. Despite a 7% drop in net sales to $4.86 billion, the company exceeded sales and profit projections, with adjusted results of $1.10 per share overcoming the $1.03 forecast.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Raises Price Target for Ecolab (ECL) to $275 | ECL S
feed.summary: Wells Fargo maintained an Equal-Weight rating on Ecolab (ECL) and raised its price target from $270 to $275, reflecting a 1.85% increase. This adjustment suggests continued confidence in Ecolab's market performance. Other recent analyst ratings for Ecolab have seen both increases and decreases in price targets, with the average target from 20 analysts suggesting a 7.20% upside from the current price, while GuruFocus estimates a 6.62% downside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Stifel Raises Price Target for Teradyne (TER) to $270 Amid Buy Rating
feed.summary: Stifel has reaffirmed its Buy rating on Teradyne (TER) and increased its price target from $225 to $270, reflecting strong confidence in the company's financial performance and market potential. This latest upgrade follows a series of positive evaluations from other firms, including B of A Securities, Goldman Sachs, and Citigroup, which have also raised their price targets for TER. Teradyne, a provider of testing equipment for semiconductors and industrial automation robots, has seen its average analyst target price reach $208.11, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $149.29, indicating a potential downside from its current price.

feed.title: MSCI Receives Maintained Rating and Increased Price Target by We
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has maintained its "Equal-Weight" rating for MSCI and increased its price target from $570 to $590, a 3.51% rise. This decision reflects continued confidence in MSCI's market position and performance potential, following similar actions by other major financial institutions. Analysts' average one-year price target for MSCI is $647.63, suggesting a 9.84% upside from its current price.

feed.title: Vistra Outperforms Industry in the Past Month: How to Play the Stock?
feed.summary: Vistra Corp. (VST) has outperformed its industry in the past month, driven by its acquisition of Cogentrix Energy and a 20-year power purchase agreement with Meta to supply clean energy. The company's focus on clean energy, strategic investments, and strong asset base position it well for future growth amidst rising demand from AI-powered data centers. While VST shows strong estimated earnings growth and a high ROE, its current premium valuation suggests new investors might wait for a better entry point.

feed.title: IBM vs. MSFT: Which Cloud & AI Tech Giant is a Better Buy?
feed.summary: This article compares IBM (IBM) and Microsoft (MSFT) as leading players in the cloud computing and AI industries, evaluating which company presents a better investment opportunity. IBM is highlighted for its hybrid cloud and AI solutions, especially with the acquisition of HashiCorp and its watsonx platform, but faces competition and margin pressure. Microsoft's Azure platform is lauded for its market share, robust ecosystem, and strategic investments in OpenAI, although its escalating capital expenditures are noted as a concern.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts Believe Uber (UBER) Could Rally 31.3%: Here's is How to Trade
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts project a potential 31.3% upside for Uber (UBER), with a mean price target of $112.14. While price targets are often viewed with skepticism, the strong agreement among analysts and positive earnings estimate revisions suggest a legitimate reason for optimism. Uber currently holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), further indicating its near-term potential.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says Cheesecake Factory Is “Way Too Cheap for a Very High-Quality Still Growth Company”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer believes Cheesecake Factory (NASDAQ: CAKE) is undervalued, calling it "way too cheap for a very high-quality still growth company" given its 13 times earnings valuation and 14% year-to-date gain. He highlighted the company's strong management, unique value proposition through its diverse menu, and impressive unit-level margins. Despite his positive sentiment, Cramer advised caution before buying, suggesting investors wait for upcoming earnings reports due to management's historically conservative outlook that has sometimes led to stock dips even after strong quarters.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Kimberly-Clark: “I Think Michael Hsu Is Doing a Fantastic Job With What the Hand He’s Got”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on Kimberly-Clark (NASDAQ: KMB), stating that CEO Michael Hsu is doing a "fantastic job" despite perceived weaknesses related to Kenvue and Procter & Gamble. He indicated that the stock, with a 5% yield and trading at 13 times earnings, seems intriguing. Cramer also discussed Kimberly-Clark's bold bid to acquire Kenvue, acknowledging the risks of lawsuits but noting the compelling value and potential for another acquirer to drive the stock up.

feed.title: MSCI (NYSE:MSCI) Stock Price Expected to Rise, Wells Fargo & Company Analyst Says
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company analysts increased their price target for MSCI (NYSE:MSCI) to $590, maintaining an "equal weight" rating, while the overall analyst consensus is a "Moderate Buy" with a $650.13 target. The company recently surpassed EPS expectations, announced a $3 billion share buyback, and reported significant insider buying by CEO Henry Fernandez. These factors, alongside positive ratings from other firms, suggest a potential upside for MSCI's stock.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) Price Target Raised to $189.00
feed.summary: Stifel Nicolaus has raised its price target for J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) to $189.00 from $172.00, while maintaining a "hold" rating, suggesting a potential downside from its current trading price. Despite insider selling, institutional ownership remains high at nearly 75%, and the company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates. J.B. Hunt's stock has seen a significant price performance, trading near its 12-month high with a market cap of $19.46 billion.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Issues Positive Forecast for FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) Stock Price
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company increased its price target for FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) stock from $260.00 to $265.00, despite maintaining an "underweight" rating, suggesting a potential downside. The article notes that FactSet recently exceeded quarterly EPS and revenue expectations, provided positive FY2026 EPS guidance, and authorized a $600 million share buyback program. Analyst consensus remains "Reduce" with an average price target of $313.00, while institutional investors have been adjusting their positions in the company.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Lowers Equifax (NYSE:EFX) Price Target to $265.00
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has lowered its price target for Equifax (NYSE:EFX) from $285.00 to $265.00, while maintaining an "overweight" rating. This adjustment still implies a potential upside of 21.36% from the company's previous close. Despite this reduction, analyst coverage remains largely positive, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average price target of $273.07.

feed.title: Stifel Nicolaus Increases Teradyne (NASDAQ:TER) Price Target to $270.00
feed.summary: Stifel Nicolaus has increased its price target for Teradyne (NASDAQ:TER) from $225.00 to $270.00, maintaining a "buy" rating and suggesting an 18.66% upside. This new target is significantly above the consensus analyst target of $200.44 for the company. Teradyne recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating expectations with an EPS of $0.85 and revenue of $769.2 million, and continues to attract attention from various institutional investors.

feed.title: Barclays Adjusts Southern Price Target to $88 From $91, Maintains Equal Weight Rating
feed.summary: Barclays has updated its price target for Southern Company (SO) to $88, down from $91, while reiterating an "Equal Weight" rating on the stock. Southern Company specializes in electricity production and distribution, with primary services including electricity distribution, natural gas distribution and marketing, and wholesale electricity sales.

feed.title: Tesla’s India Letdown Spurs Discounts on Unsold Model Y SUVs
feed.summary: Tesla is reportedly facing sluggish demand for its Model Y SUVs in India, with about a third of its initial imports remaining unsold four months after shipment. This has led the company to offer discounts of up to 200,000 rupees ($2,200) on some variants to clear the inventory. The issue arises as some early bookers have withdrawn their purchases, leaving approximately 100 vehicles without buyers.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Radiant Logistics: “If You’re Going to Be in That Business, You Want to Be in FedEx”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer advised that while Radiant Logistics (NYSE: RLGT) is an interesting company in the logistics business, investors interested in that sector should look to FedEx (FDX) instead due to its established logistics capabilities. Radiant Logistics provides global transportation services for various industries. The article suggests that certain AI stocks may offer better upside potential with less risk than RLGT.

feed.title: Bernstein Reduces Firm’s PT Diamondback Energy (FANG) Stock
feed.summary: Bernstein has reduced its price target for Diamondback Energy (FANG) stock to $190 from $199, while maintaining an "Outperform" rating, citing an expectation of near-term choppiness but long-term strength for oil. Concurrently, Piper Sandler reiterated an "Overweight (Buy)" rating with a $219 price objective, highlighting Diamondback Energy as a top large-cap E&P due to its low-cost operations in the Permian and consistent execution. The article also suggests that while FANG has potential, certain AI stocks might offer greater upside and less risk.

feed.title: Ecarx to receive $45.6m investment from Geely Holding
feed.summary: Automotive technology provider Ecarx Holdings has secured a $45.6 million strategic investment from Chinese automotive conglomerate Geely Holding through a private placement. Geely will purchase approximately 27.29 million newly issued Class A ordinary shares at $1.67 per share. This investment aims to bolster Ecarx's global expansion, R&D capabilities, and infrastructure across key growth markets.

feed.title: BorgWarner, Black Married Workers Agree to End Harassment Suit
feed.summary: BorgWarner and two married former Black employees have agreed to settle a lawsuit alleging racial harassment. Christopher and Latonia Morris had claimed a manager subjected them to a racially hostile work environment, leading to their termination which BorgWarner stated was due to workplace arguments and harassing other employees. The dismissal stipulation was filed in the US District Court for the Northern District of Illinois after the parties reached a settlement.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical Falls as Rivalry Slows Down Flagship Robot
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical Inc. shares experienced a significant drop after the company projected slower growth for its da Vinci surgical robotic system in 2026. This forecast, attributed to increasing competition in the market, falls below analyst estimates and would represent the slowest growth rate since 2020.

feed.title: YieldBoost Boise Cascade To 18.4% Using Options
feed.summary: Boise Cascade Co. (BCC) shareholders can enhance their annual return to 18.4% by selling the April covered call at the $90 strike, which offers an additional 17.4% on top of the stock's 1.1% dividend yield. This strategy provides a significant boost, though any stock appreciation above $90 would be forfeited if the shares are called away. The article notes that BCC would need to advance 8.8% for the stock to be called, resulting in a 13.2% return from the current trading level, plus any dividends.

feed.title: Expeditors International stock hits all-time high at 161.9 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Expeditors International of Washington Inc. (EXPD) stock reached an all-time high of 161.9 USD, driven by a 46.21% surge over the past year and strong operational performance. The logistics company has maintained dividend payments for 33 consecutive years, and management is actively buying back shares. Analysts from Goldman Sachs, BofA Securities, and UBS have upgraded their ratings and raised price targets, reflecting a positive outlook on the company's future growth.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries to Acquire Synopsys’ Processor IP Solutions Business to Accelerate Physical AI Applications
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) announced its definitive agreement to acquire Synopsys’ ARC Processor IP Solutions business, including its engineering teams and product lines like ARC-V and ARC NPX NPU. This acquisition, expected to close in the second half of 2026, will integrate with GF's MIPS company to enhance custom silicon solutions and accelerate GF's Physical AI roadmap, particularly for applications in wearables, robotics, and advanced AI silicon. Both companies anticipate this move will strengthen their respective focuses: GF in end-to-end AI solutions and Synopsys in core interface and foundation IP.

feed.title: Boeing delivered 600 commercial aircraft in 2025
feed.summary: Boeing delivered 600 commercial aircraft in 2025, a 72.4% increase year-over-year, and recorded 1,175 commercial gross orders, beating rival Airbus in orders for the first time since 2018. While Boeing outpaced Airbus in orders, Airbus still led in overall deliveries. The company expects increased deliveries in 2026 for its 737 and 787 models, which is anticipated to be a significant driver of positive cash flow.

feed.title: Women’s Motocross Championship joins SMX for 2026 with Synchrony as presenting sponsor
feed.summary: The Women’s Motocross Championship (WMX) is set to join the SuperMotocross (SMX) League in 2026, with Synchrony as a presenting sponsor. This integration will see the WMX season feature six rounds alongside the AMA Pro Racing-sanctioned Pro Motocross Championship, concluding with a showcase race at the Monster Energy SMX World Championship Final. The partnership aims to boost visibility and participation for women riders, leveraging national broadcast coverage on Peacock and international access via the SMX Video Pass.

feed.title: Analysts Are Bullish on Top Technology Stocks: Core Scientific Inc (CORZ), Frequency Electronics (FEIM)
feed.summary: Two analysts have issued bullish sentiments on technology stocks Core Scientific Inc (CORZ) and Frequency Electronics (FEIM). Craig-Hallum maintained a Buy rating on Core Scientific Inc, with a strong buy consensus and a price target suggesting significant upside. Craig-Hallum also maintained a Buy rating on Frequency Electronics, although its consensus is a Moderate Buy.

feed.title: Inside Fat Brands’ ‘complicated’ debt situation
feed.summary: Fat Brands is grappling with a "complicated" debt situation totaling $1.3 billion, stemming from various acquisitions and an inability to refinance due to rising interest rates and a prior fraud investigation. CEO Andy Wiederhorn is negotiating with numerous creditors across five different securitization trusts to restructure the non-recourse debt, exploring both in-court and out-of-court solutions. Despite these financial challenges, the company reports relatively stable same-store sales and strong operational performance compared to some competitors.

feed.title: Allergic Rhinitis Market to Experience Notable Growth in Forecast Span by 2034, DelveInsight Predicts | Regeneron Pharma, Allergy Therapeutics, Inmunotek, UCB Pharma, Orexo AB, Hyloris Pharma
feed.summary: The global Allergic Rhinitis market is projected to reach approximately USD 10,400 million in 2023 and is expected to grow significantly by 2034, driven by increasing prevalence and the launch of new therapies. The US market was the largest in 2023, followed by Japan and Germany, with a total of nearly 90 million diagnosed prevalent cases across the 7MM. Key companies like Regeneron Pharmaceuticals and Allergy Therapeutics are developing new treatments that are expected to revolutionize market dynamics.

feed.title: Chipotle brand chief Chris Brandt steps down
feed.summary: Chipotle's President and Chief Brand Officer, Chris Brandt, has stepped down from his role, effective January 12, 2026, though he will remain in an advisory capacity for a limited time. Stephanie Perdue, VP of brand marketing, will serve as interim CMO while the company searches for a new chief marketing officer. Brandt is credited with helping Chipotle become a "purpose-driven lifestyle brand" during his nearly eight-year tenure.

feed.title: JBHT: Stifel Maintains Hold Rating, Increases Price Target to $189
feed.summary: Stifel has maintained its "Hold" rating for JB Hunt Transport Services (JBHT) but raised its price target from $172 to $189. This adjustment reflects an optimistic outlook despite the unchanged rating. Other analysts have also recently updated their ratings and price targets for JBHT, with the average analyst target price suggesting a slight downside from the current price.

feed.title: Teledyne acquires UK gas sensor maker DD-Scientific By Investing.com
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies (NYSE:TDY) has acquired DD-Scientific Holdings Limited, a UK-based manufacturer of electrochemical gas sensors. The acquisition strengthens Teledyne's portfolio in industrial safety, healthcare, and environmental monitoring, adding specialized technology like lead-free sensors. Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed, but Teledyne emphasized its strong financial position and continued strategic expansion.

feed.title: Travelers’ CTO on “perform and transform” – and the discipline behind insurance innovation
feed.summary: Mojgan Lefebvre, EVP and CTO at Travelers, discusses the company's "perform and transform" strategy, which balances reliable execution with continuous innovation in a cloud- and AI-driven market. She explains how modernization focuses on architectural upgrades, data foundation improvements, and strategic partnerships. Lefebvre emphasizes rethinking processes and a collaborative approach to ensure technology transformation aligns with business objectives.

feed.title: Agnico thinks big at Hope Bay in Nunavut
feed.summary: Agnico Eagle Mines Ltd. is significantly investing in its Hope Bay project in Nunavut, aiming to transform it into a major gold producer with an annual output of at least 400,000 ounces. This expansion follows the company's acquisition of TMAC Resources and focuses on the high-grade Madrid deposit, alongside continued exploration and development at its existing Meliadine and Meadowbank mines in the territory. A prefeasibility study for Hope Bay is expected by year-end, with drill results from Madrid showing strong potential for deeper high-grade mineralization.

feed.title: The Coca-Cola Company Announces Timing of Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Earnings Release; Company Also Announces Presentation at Consumer Analyst Group of New York (CAGNY) Conference
feed.summary: The Coca-Cola Company has scheduled its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results release for February 10, to be followed by an investor conference call. Additionally, CEO-elect Henrique Braun and CFO John Murphy will present at the Consumer Analyst Group of New York (CAGNY) Conference on February 17. Investors can access webcasts for both events via the company's investor relations website.

feed.title: Is Boeing's Q4 Delivery Growth Indicating Better Execution?
feed.summary: Boeing recently released its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 delivery figures, showing a significant increase in commercial aircraft deliveries and a steady performance in its defense segment. The company delivered 160 commercial airplanes in Q4 and 600 for the full year, indicating improved production execution and cash flow. This growth, coupled with a diversified portfolio, signals stronger operational control and customer confidence, even as competitors like Airbus maintain higher overall delivery volumes.

feed.title: Ahead of Earnings, Is Charles Schwab Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
feed.summary: Ahead of Charles Schwab's (SCHW) Q4 2026 earnings release on Jan. 21, Morningstar analysts consider the stock fairly valued with a 3-star rating and a $109 fair value estimate. Key areas to watch include annual guidance, net interest margin, net new asset growth, advisor headcount, and the launch of new digital products like cryptocurrency trading. Schwab maintains a wide economic moat due to its cost advantage and strong financial position, though it faces risks related to interest rate sensitivity and potential regulatory changes.

feed.title: INVESTOR NOTICE: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Investigates Claims on Behalf of Investors of F5
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is investigating potential claims against F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) on behalf of investors who suffered losses between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025. The investigation follows allegations that F5 made misleading statements and failed to disclose a significant security breach affecting its key product, BIG-IP, which subsequently led to a dramatic drop in F5's stock price. Investors have until February 17, 2026, to seek the role of lead plaintiff in the federal securities class action.

feed.title: Microsoft’s spending on Anthropic AI on track to reach $500M: report
feed.summary: Microsoft is reportedly on track to spend approximately $500 million annually on Anthropic AI to integrate it into its products, making it one of Anthropic's top customers. This strategic move diversifies Microsoft's AI technology sources, strengthens its enterprise offerings against competitors, and supplements its existing partnership with OpenAI. Microsoft has also committed a significant investment of up to $5 billion in Anthropic, with Anthropic agreeing to spend $30 billion on Azure compute services.

feed.title: Rockwell Automation (NYSE:ROK) Lowered to "Hold" Rating by Zacks Research
feed.summary: Zacks Research downgraded Rockwell Automation (NYSE:ROK) from a "strong-buy" to a "hold" rating, citing high valuation despite strong financial performance. Rockwell recently exceeded quarterly earnings expectations and saw a 13.8% year-over-year revenue increase. The company's average price target from other analysts is $406.44, with the stock currently trading around $416.80.

feed.title: Did A US$32 Million 2026 Rate Hike Approval Just Shift American States Water's (AWR) Investment Narrative?
feed.summary: American States Water Company's subsidiary, Golden State Water Company, received California Public Utilities Commission approval for a US$32.0 million rate increase in 2026. This approval supports the company's near-term earnings outlook but introduces higher earnings volatility due to the loss of full revenue decoupling. The company projects modest revenue and earnings growth by 2028, with a fair value indicating a 10% upside.

feed.title: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) Now Covered by BNP Paribas Exane
feed.summary: BNP Paribas Exane initiated coverage on Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) with an "outperform" rating and a $108 price target, indicating a potential 27.31% upside. This follows a strong earnings report by Uber, which saw EPS of $3.11 against an expected $0.67 and revenue up 20.4% year-over-year. Despite recent insider stock sales, the company holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with an average price target of $107.18.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Reaffirms "Neutral" Rating for Controladora Vuela Compania de Aviacion (NYSE:VLRS)
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has reiterated its "neutral" rating for Controladora Vuela Compania de Aviacion (NYSE:VLRS) and increased its price target to $11.00, suggesting a potential 20% upside. Other analysts have a mixed view, with a MarketBeat consensus of "Hold" and an average price target of $9.22, while some firms have recently upgraded the stock and raised targets. The transportation company's stock is currently trading around $9.16 with a market capitalization of $1.08 billion and negative profitability metrics.

feed.title: Regency Centers price target lowered to $76 from $78 at Scotiabank
feed.summary: Scotiabank has lowered its price target for Regency Centers (REG) to $76 from $78, while maintaining a Sector Perform rating. This adjustment comes as the firm updates its price targets on U.S. Real Estate & REITs ahead of the Q4 earnings season. Scotiabank notes increasing positive sentiment toward Self Storage and Multifamily subsectors, anticipating strong FY26 guidance from both.

feed.title: Is Earnings Anticipation and Software Momentum Altering The Investment Case For Mettler-Toledo International (MTD)?
feed.summary: Mettler-Toledo International Inc. (MTD) is garnering investor attention ahead of its Q4 2025 earnings release on February 5, 2026. The company's investment case is largely tied to long-term shifts towards automation, data-driven labs, and regulated quality control, with immediate focus on how recurring software and services revenue will counter tariff-related margin pressures. Analysts expect modest EPS growth, making the upcoming earnings call crucial for assessing both opportunities and risks.

feed.title: Does Coca-Cola’s (KO) Digital Reshuffle and Costa Bet Quietly Redefine Its Core Strategy?
feed.summary: Coca-Cola recently altered its leadership structure by appointing a Chief Digital Officer and realigning regional responsibilities, alongside its decision to retain Costa Coffee after failing to receive satisfactory auction bids. This strategic repositioning signals a deeper commitment to digital engagement and a refinement of its portfolio mix, particularly ahead of upcoming earnings. For investors, these moves highlight a push for execution in emerging markets and a focused approach to growth, though ongoing concerns about sugar regulations and consumer health preferences remain.

feed.title: NVIDIA Corp., Goldman Sachs share losses lead Dow's 216-point fall
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 216 points, primarily due to significant share losses from NVIDIA Corp. and Goldman Sachs. This decline indicates a notable pullback in the market, influenced by the performance of these major companies. The article likely details the specific factors contributing to their respective share depreciations and the broader market impact.

feed.title: What 13.6M U.S. car buyers reveal about Tesla, GM and brand loyalty
feed.summary: S&P Global Mobility announced the winners of its 30th annual Automotive Loyalty Awards, with General Motors securing 'Overall Loyalty to Manufacturer' for the 11th consecutive year and Tesla winning 'Overall Loyalty to Make' for the fourth year. The awards are based on an analysis of 13.6 million new U.S. retail vehicle registrations. The data highlights diverse strategies for customer retention, including product redesigns for models like the Chevrolet Equinox, targeted market appeal for Tesla, and strong dealer performance for Subaru.
